run_name: NATP Module 10 Vital Signs — Source-First Dry Run
purpose: Prepare students to know how, when and why vital signs are taken; report and chart procedures; measure temperature, pulse, respirations, and blood pressure; recognize and report normal and abnormal findings.

objectives.weight_reason: User-approved weighted allocation based on source content density; assessment, clinical, certificate, and optional support time excluded.
source_density_basis: review listed terminology
spell/pronounce terms accurately
use terms in proper charting/reporting context
objectives.excluded_clinical_minutes: Source recommends 6 clinical hours for module overall; deferred to in-person/evaluator-supported work and not counted here.
content_outline: review listed terminology
spell/pronounce terms accurately
use terms in proper charting/reporting context
recommended_teaching_strategies: Games: word searches, crossword puzzles, Family Feud, Jeopardy, bingo, spelling bee, hangman, concentration
handouts: Handout 10.1b Vital Signs Crossword Key (internal/instructor key)
objectives.sample_test_items: Module 10 sample test pages 17-22 include related items; answer key page 23 is internal-only.
safety_reporting_charting_scope_boundari: CNA observes/measures/records/reports within facility policy; abnormal findings and complaints are reported to licensed nurse; no online hands-on competency validation is claimed.
media_opportunities: mobile diagram/visual placeholder/source-traced prompt only until generated assets approved

objectives.objective_title: Discuss the use of temperature as an indicator of body function
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Discuss the use of temperature as an indicator of body function
objectives.weight_reason: User-approved weighted allocation based on source content density; assessment, clinical, certificate, and optional support time excluded.
source_density_basis: temperature balances heat gained/lost
hypothalamus regulates temperature
heat production via cellular activity, metabolism, muscle activity, hormones
heat loss via skin, lungs, elimination
heat conservation by reduced perspiration/blood flow and shivering
oral, rectal, tympanic, axillary, temporal sites
glass/digital/electronic/disposable/tympanic/temporal thermometers
normal ranges and route differences
contraindications and abnormal reporting
objectives.excluded_clinical_minutes: Source recommends 6 clinical hours for module overall; deferred to in-person/evaluator-supported work and not counted here.
content_outline: temperature balances heat gained/lost
hypothalamus regulates temperature
heat production via cellular activity, metabolism, muscle activity, hormones
heat loss via skin, lungs, elimination
heat conservation by reduced perspiration/blood flow and shivering
oral, rectal, tympanic, axillary, temporal sites
glass/digital/electronic/disposable/tympanic/temporal thermometers
normal ranges and route differences
contraindications and abnormal reporting
recommended_teaching_strategies: Demonstration/return demonstration with thermometers and contraindications

objectives.objective_title: Describe factors that increase and decrease pulse, and the qualities to observe in taking a pulse
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe factors that increase and decrease pulse, and the qualities to observe in taking a pulse
objectives.weight_reason: User-approved weighted allocation based on source content density; assessment, clinical, certificate, and optional support time excluded.
source_density_basis: increase pulse: exercise, fever, hemorrhage, pain, shock, strong emotions, tachycardia over 100
decrease pulse: sleep/rest, depression, drugs, athlete conditioning, bradycardia below 60
qualities: rate, rhythm, strength/force, thready, bounding
report irregular rhythm even if rate normal
objectives.excluded_clinical_minutes: Source recommends 6 clinical hours for module overall; deferred to in-person/evaluator-supported work and not counted here.
content_outline: increase pulse: exercise, fever, hemorrhage, pain, shock, strong emotions, tachycardia over 100
decrease pulse: sleep/rest, depression, drugs, athlete conditioning, bradycardia below 60
qualities: rate, rhythm, strength/force, thready, bounding
report irregular rhythm even if rate normal
recommended_teaching_strategies: Demonstration/return demonstration for radial/apical pulse full minute
clinical_demonstration_method_of_evaluat: Measure radial/brachial pulse for full minute
manual_skills: Manual Skill Count and Record Radial Pulse

objectives.objective_title: Describe the process for taking TPR as a combined procedure
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe the process for taking TPR as a combined procedure
objectives.weight_reason: User-approved weighted allocation based on source content density; assessment, clinical, certificate, and optional support time excluded.
source_density_basis: take temperature first, pulse second, respirations third
remember pulse while counting respirations
do not stop to record pulse before respirations
report abnormal findings
